title: ECDSA composite public keys allocate only one coordinate
problem: `CRYPT_CTRL_GET_BITS` returns the curve size in bits, but `CRYPT_PARAM_EC_PUBKEY` exports the full encoded EC point. This allocates 32/48/66 bytes for P-256/P-384/P-521 instead of 65/97/133, so `getPub()` fails and every ECDSA-based composite keygen/export path breaks.

title: Reinitializing a composite context can overflow stale key buffers
problem: `CRYPT_CompositeSetAlgInfo()` can be called multiple times on the same context. It replaces `ctx->info` and allocates new subcontexts, but `CRYPT_CompositeCreateKeyBuf()` only allocates when `pubKey`/`prvKey` are `NULL`. If the context already holds a smaller key and is reconfigured to a larger algorithm, `CRYPT_COMPOSITE_GenKey()` copies the larger key into the old allocation. Even before keygen, repeated initialization leaks the old `pqcCtx` and `tradCtx`.

title: `CRYPT_CTRL_SET_RSA_E` is ignored after parameter setup
problem: The new control only updates `ctx->e`, but the RSA subcontext consumes that value only once inside `CRYPT_CompositeSetAlgInfo()`. In the normal call order (`PkeySetParaById` then `PkeyCtrl(...SET_RSA_E...)` then `PkeyGen`), the generated key still uses the default exponent `65537`.

title: MLDSA87/P-384 private-key length table has a misplaced digit
problem: The max composite private-key length for `CRYPT_COMPOSITE_MLDSA87_ECDSA_P384_SHA512` is recorded as `962` instead of `96`. `CRYPT_COMPOSITE_SetPrvKey()` uses this table entry as its upper bound, so malformed inputs with a valid 96-byte key plus trailing garbage are accepted.
code:
{CRYPT_COMPOSITE_MLDSA87_ECDSA_P384_SHA512, "COMPSIG-MLDSA87-ECDSA-P384-SHA512",
 CRYPT_PKEY_ML_DSA, CRYPT_MLDSA_TYPE_MLDSA_87, CRYPT_PKEY_ECDSA, CRYPT_ECC_NISTP384,
 CRYPT_MD_SHA512, CRYPT_MD_SHA384, 0, 2689, 962, 2592, 32, 4627},
fix:
{CRYPT_COMPOSITE_MLDSA87_ECDSA_P384_SHA512, "COMPSIG-MLDSA87-ECDSA-P384-SHA512",
 CRYPT_PKEY_ML_DSA, CRYPT_MLDSA_TYPE_MLDSA_87, CRYPT_PKEY_ECDSA, CRYPT_ECC_NISTP384,
 CRYPT_MD_SHA512, CRYPT_MD_SHA384, 0, 2689, 96, 2592, 32, 4627},

title: Ed448 composite OIDs are routed to an unimplemented parser
problem: The new PKCS#8 and SPKI dispatch tables treat `BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ED448_SHAKE256` as a supported composite key type, but the composite implementation has no `g_composite_info` entry or traditional-key handler for Ed448. Any key with this OID is advertised as supported and then fails during `CRYPT_EAL_PkeySetParaById()`.
code:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_BRAINPOOLP384R1_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ED448_SHAKE256:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA3072_PSS_SHA512:
...
    return ParseCompositePrikeyAsn1Buff(...);

case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_BRAINPOOLP384R1_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ED448_SHAKE256:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA3072_PSS_SHA512:
...
    return ParseCompositePubkeyAsn1Buff(...);
fix:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_BRAINPOOLP384R1_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA3072_PSS_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA4096_PSS_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_P521_SHA512:
    return ParseCompositePrikeyAsn1Buff(...);

case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_BRAINPOOLP384R1_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA3072_PSS_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_RSA4096_PSS_SHA512:
case BSL_CID_MLDSA87_ECDSA_P521_SHA512:
    return ParseCompositePubkeyAsn1Buff(...);
